name: send_whatsapp_approval
description: Send one human approval request over the WhatsApp Business API — the approval channel for both the site-deploy gate and the per-post publish gate, alongside Hermes' existing web/Telegram/Discord channel identities. Supports single-item requests (one client's site_deploy, or one post's post_publish_single) and batch fan-out requests (post_publish_batch, a 'reply ALL' covering two or more of one client's already-voice_qa-passing posts) — for a batch, this tool sends exactly one WhatsApp message but returns one distinct approval_requests document id per artifact_ref, never a single shared id covering the batch; the caller records each id individually. This tool only SENDS the request template (client-scoped, per FLEET ISOLATION — never references or blocks on another client's state); the human's reply is captured by a separate inbound webhook (not part of this manifest) that resolves each returned approval_request_id to granted/denied. Every approval this tool issues is single-use per artifact_ref and is never reused across a retry of the same site/post or across a different client. Fails clearly (fail-loud, non-retryable message) when WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN, WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID, or WHATSAPP_BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_ID is unavailable, or when the client has no recipient WhatsApp identity on file, rather than silently degrading or fabricating a request id.

client_id.description: Client this approval request is scoped to; threaded through to output and downstream fleet events. Never inferred — must be passed through explicitly per FLEET ISOLATION. This tool must never read or block on another client_id's approval state.

fleet_id.description: ID of the parent fleet run this approval request belongs to, if this call is part of a fleet-run pass (site-build). Omit for a standalone content-loop cycle not tied to a fleet run.

approval_kind.description: Which gate this request authorizes. 'site_deploy' precedes exactly one deploy_site call. 'post_publish_single' precedes exactly one publish_x/publish_linkedin pair for one draft_id. 'post_publish_batch' precedes a WhatsApp 'reply ALL' fan-out covering multiple draft_ids, every one of which must already carry a passing voice_qa_report before this call is made — batch approval is a review convenience only and is never available for a post whose voice_qa_report isn't already gate_result: pass.

phone_number_id.description: WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID this request is sent from (the Auctor agency's own WhatsApp Business sender identity, not the client's).

recipient.description: Human approver's WhatsApp number/identity on file for this client. Required — this tool fails loud rather than falling back to a default or shared recipient.

summary.description: Short human-readable summary of what's being approved: for site_deploy, the site preview link and client name; for post_publish_single, the post text preview; for post_publish_batch, a numbered list of each draft's text preview so a single 'reply ALL' can be understood at a glance.

artifact_refs.description: build_id (site_deploy: exactly one) or draft_id (post_publish_single: exactly one; post_publish_batch: two or more). Every ref must already carry a passing voice_qa_report (gate_result: pass) for its exact candidate_ref before this call is made — this tool does not itself check voice_qa_report, the calling specialist (deployer or publisher) is responsible for only invoking it with already-passing candidates.

message_id.description: WhatsApp Business API message id for the single outbound message sent (one message even for a batch request).

approval_request_ids.description: One approval_requests document id per entry in artifact_refs, in the same order, even for a post_publish_batch request — never a single shared id covering the batch. Each id is what a later agency_approve/agency_approve_batch call resolves once the human replies.

error_message.description: Present only when status is 'failed', e.g. 'missing_api_key', 'unlinked_recipient', or a WhatsApp API error detail.

sent_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the message was accepted by the WhatsApp Business API.

usage.description: Provider usage/cost for this send, rolled up into ClientPipeline.usage then the fleet-level total, per artifacts.md's usage-rollup rule.

tokens_in.description: Always 0 for this tool — WhatsApp send is a template API call, not an LLM call.

cost_usd.description: Per-message WhatsApp Business API conversation cost, if billed by the provider for this template/recipient/category.

side_effects: Sends a real outbound WhatsApp Business API message to the client's approver, consuming that provider's send/conversation quota and, where applicable, billed conversation cost.
notes: This tool's own risk_level (medium) and approval_policy ('none') are deliberately lower than the gates it feeds (deploy_site and publish_x/publish_linkedin are both risk_level 'high' with a required, single-use, per-client-or-per-post approval_policy) — sending a REQUEST for approval is not itself the production-affecting action, and does not require a prior approval to invoke. The human's reply is captured by a separate inbound WhatsApp webhook (not part of this manifest) that resolves each approval_request_id in approval_request_ids to granted/denied independently; a 'reply ALL' inbound resolves every id in a post_publish_batch request independently, never as one shared grant, per policy.md's APPROVAL section.
